best_for: Any user-facing chat or generation UI — perceived latency drops dramatically when tokens render as they arrive
avoid_when: Backend-to-backend calls where nothing renders incrementally
tradeoffs: Better UX; slightly more code (SSE plumbing, partial-message handling, client abort)

best_for: Short completions, background jobs, structured extraction consumed by code not humans
avoid_when: Long generations a user watches — waiting 20s for a wall of text feels broken
tradeoffs: Simplest possible integration; fine whenever no human stares at a spinner

best_for: Letting the model query your DB, call your APIs, or take actions through a curated tool list
avoid_when: Exposing broad/destructive capabilities without human confirmation gates
tradeoffs: Turns the model into an orchestrator; every tool is attack surface — scope to least privilege (AI031)

best_for: Grounding answers in private/current data: docs, tickets, product catalogs — pgvector on Supabase/Postgres is the default fit here
avoid_when: Data that fits comfortably in the prompt (just include it); ungoverned corpora (see AI041)
tradeoffs: Answers reflect your data not training data; adds chunking, embedding, and retrieval-quality tuning work

best_for: Corpora with structure: chunk by semantic unit (heading/section), embed titles with bodies, retrieve more then rerank
avoid_when: Skipping evaluation — bad retrieval silently produces confident wrong answers
tradeoffs: Retrieval quality caps answer quality; measure hit-rate on a golden question set before tuning prompts

best_for: Multi-turn products: store messages in your own DB keyed by conversation, replay a token-budgeted window per call
tradeoffs: You own history, deletion, and export (KVKK/GDPR); replayed history is the main input-cost driver — pair with caching (AI010) and summarization (AI013)

best_for: System prompts as code: reviewed in PRs, versioned, eval-gated before rollout
avoid_when: Prompts hidden in dashboards or DB rows nobody reviews
tradeoffs: Prompt changes become diffable and testable; requires the discipline to run evals on every change

best_for: Handling 429/529 and transient errors: exponential backoff, then optionally degrade to a cheaper model to stay up
avoid_when: Retrying non-idempotent side-effectful agent actions blindly
tradeoffs: Keeps the feature alive through rate limits and incidents; fallback answers may be visibly weaker

option: Eval suite on a golden set
best_for: Every prompt/model change: a fixed set of real inputs with expected properties, run in CI or pre-deploy
tradeoffs: Catches regressions humans miss; building a representative golden set is real work — start with 20 cases

best_for: Scaling evals beyond exact-match: a second model grades outputs against a rubric
avoid_when: Treating judge scores as ground truth without spot-checking against human labels
tradeoffs: Cheap large-scale grading; judges share model biases — calibrate on a human-labeled sample first

best_for: High-traffic features where many users ask near-identical questions (FAQ bots, product Q&A)
avoid_when: Personalized or context-dependent answers where cache hits would leak or mislead
tradeoffs: Can eliminate a large share of calls; cache-key design (embedding similarity threshold) is the hard part

best_for: Start every feature as one well-prompted call; split into orchestrated sub-calls only when one prompt provably can't do the job
avoid_when: Multi-agent as the default architecture — it multiplies cost, latency, and failure modes
tradeoffs: Simplest thing that works wins; agents compound errors across steps unless each has evals

best_for: Almost every product need: instruction-following on frontier models with good prompts and grounding beats most fine-tunes
avoid_when: Genuine style/format specialization at scale after prompting is exhausted
tradeoffs: No training pipeline to own; fine-tuning adds data prep, drift, and re-training cost for narrow wins
